author: @orpheuscreativeco9236
text:
This objectification you speak of is a huge issue in religion, even in Buddhism. These philosophies (religions) are flawed with a mechanism that externalizes the responsibility of the individual and pins causality on fate rather than the actor themselves. (I included Buddhism because I began journeying down that path, hoping that it had something more wise and honest to offer than the other options, but it too has many internal contradictions.) That's not to say that there doe not also exist truth amd wisdom, rather that there is something to glean from any teaching, though one must be discerning in what they choose to keep and what is chaff. 

Eating meat is a fine example of what you're describing. Beef instead of cow, pork instead of pig. Hell, Pink Floyd even hit the nail on the head with "Us and Them." War is possible thanks to mental gymnastics just the same. 

Great post Rob! I'd love to read that gentleman's book ✌️

author: @VeniceInventors
text: I wish such words of wisdom could be spoken on a channel with millions of subscribers. Most of the people watching your videos may have some kind of engineer mindset and are already likely to appreciate the beauty in nature's complexity and thus want to protect it. Or at least that's what I think is the logical outcome when becoming aware of how finely tuned and interdependent this circular ecosystem is. Still, speaking out is most important, adds up and spreads out in the long run, like planting seeds every day.

author: @marcfruchtman9473
text:
We do need to be good stewards of our environment. But a great deal of the early part of the talk is about "Nature". But "Nature" itself tends to have an unusual opinion. Nature allows for fish in the sea to eat other fish in the sea. There is really no end to how willing nature is to allow one creature to "survive" by eating other creatures in nature. If we as predators over utilize our resources to the point of our own detriment we will hurt ourselves. But, we as humans see Beauty in Nature. We want to care about nature because it goes against our love of nature to see it be destroyed by our pollution. 
   So I want to be clear... We love nature... but in reality Nature allows Plants and Animals to feed on and eat other plants and animals for millions and millions of years... over and over... and "Nature" hasn't cared one bit about that. So, I think it is important to understand that we really can't stop that cycle until we find some path to enlightenment that magically fixes that. And I don't see that happening any time soon. So, with that... I am still going to eat my food... since I can't magically find energy from non-food sources.

author: @haskelswain1842
text: Interesting thoughts as always. I don't always agree but would still engage in such conversation and possibly persuade or be persuaded. So with that said I would prefer if you didn't use such words/phrases such as raping the land or useing and abusing.  An honest conversation aimed at informing or being informed cannot uses language like that. That language ends conversation. Makes me want to turn you immediately. Are you right, maybe but if I've been turned off you don't have the chance to persuade me anymore. So if you right then the only way for me to learn is to feel I have to let you offend me. I hope that came out right. Just being honest because I realy like all your channels and don't want turned off. Thank you

replies.author: @jeffrice6745
replies.text: The way humans as a species treats other animals and ecology on the whole is a horror show of terrors. Driving the world to extinction and being oblivious is awful.

replies.author: @marcfruchtman9473
replies.text: @jeffrice6745And what about the millions of fish eating the millions of other species of fish... every day over millions and millions of years... why don't they get any focus? It almost seems like a silly argument. But that is "nature". The horror is that we are hurting ourselves in the process. But nature goes on with the process of consuming itself over and over.

replies.author: @jeffrice6745
replies.text: @marcfruchtman9473 so human morality is the equivalent of fish eating fish?
